title: Uruguayan Pinto Bean Empanadas
description: A kid-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring pinto beans in a soffritto-base filling, wrapped in a crispy empanada crust, perfect for a snack or lunch. This recipe is FODMAP-friendly and has a winey, snappy flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the chopped red bell pepper and cook until tender.
Add the cooked pinto beans, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the pinto bean filling onto the center of each empanada dough circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of olive oil.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
